event: Subjective fevers, anorexia, nasal discharge and congestion, abdominal pain
Denies cough, sore throat, throat pain, hoarseness
Chest radiography: small bilateral pleural effusions
CT of abdomen and pelvis: multiple cystic lesions in liver, intrahepatic and extrahepatic bile duct dilatation, mild splenomegaly, right sided Bartholin gland cyst
Ultrasound of abdomen: hepatic abscesses, reactive gall bladder thickening
Blood cultures: growth of Fusobacterium necrophorum
Abdominal exam: mild right upper quadrant abdominal tenderness
Genitourinary exam: erythema and edema of the labia
CT of neck: no evidence of abscess formation
CT of chest: multiple nodular foci in right lung fields
Interval worsening of hepatic abscess burden
Ultrasound guided thoracentesis of right lung
Removal of 800 mL of maroon colored pleural fluid
Analysis of pleural fluid: exudative fluid
Repeat CT of chest, abdomen and pelvis: bilateral multifocal pneumonia, pleural effusions
Incision and drainage of right labial abscess
Contrast CT of abdomen and pelvis: nonocclusive thrombus in right common iliac vein
Transthoracic and transesophageal echocardiography: no evidence of valvular vegetations
Diagnosis of atypical Lemierre syndrome of urogenital origin
Transitioned to intravenous ceftriaxone and oral metronidazole
